identity.name: Nexus Athletics
identity.core_philosophy: Fusion over Imitation. Mix tactical military precision with cyberpunk neon aesthetics.

dashboard_grid.instructions: Use Flexbox with 'flexWrap: wrap'. Create a container with 'flexDirection: row'. Children should have percentage widths (e.g., '48%' for 2-col, '100%' for hero). Use 'gap' or margins for spacing.

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use 'expo-blur' (BlurView) with intensity 20-30 and tint 'dark' for overlays and floating headers.
visual_enhancers.gradients: Use 'expo-linear-gradient' for button backgrounds or subtle glows behind hero images.
visual_enhancers.borders: Use 1px borders with low opacity (10-20%) white to define edges in dark mode. Do not rely solely on shadows.

image_urls.logo_placeholder: Use text 'NEXUS' in bold font if no logo image is available.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: This is a React Native (Expo) project. DO NOT use HTML tags (div, span, h1). Use View, Text, TouchableOpacity, ScrollView.
CRITICAL: DO NOT use Tailwind CSS classes (e.g., className='p-4'). They will not work. Use the 'style' prop with StyleSheet objects.
Use 'SafeAreaView' from 'react-native-safe-area-context' for all screen containers.
Implement the 'Bento Grid' layout using Flexbox logic (flexDirection: row, flexWrap: wrap).
Use 'expo-linear-gradient' for the primary action buttons to give them a neon glow feel.
Ensure all text has sufficient contrast against the #0A0A0A background. Default text color should be #A1A1AA, headings #FFFFFF.
For the 'EntrenadorIA' (Chat), use a bubble design: User (Green bg, Black text), AI (Dark Grey bg, White text).
Add 'data-testid' props to all interactive elements for testing.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Avoid generic 'flat' designs. Use borders, subtle gradients, and blur to create depth.
Do not center everything. Use left alignment for text to improve readability.
Use 2-3x more spacing than typical. 'padding: 24' is better than 'padding: 10'.
Every interaction needs feedback. Use 'activeOpacity={0.7}' on TouchableOpacity.
Dark colors look good when used independently. Avoid muddy gradients.
